preamble: Glottal reinforcement of [ε] at word onset involves adding [ʔ] before the vowel rather than replacing it. True ε→ʔ replacement (where the vowel is lost and only [ʔ] remains) is rare; most examples show hard attack (∅→ʔ + ε). In some languages with laryngealized vowels, an [ε] can be accompanied by creaky voice transitioning to a full glottal closure.

examples.note: Glottal onset: [ʔ] added BEFORE [ε], not replacing it. Strictly ∅→ʔ, not ε→ʔ; listed here as glottal reinforcement of vowel-initial words.

sources: Ladefoged, P. & Johnson, K. (2015). A Course in Phonetics (7th ed.). Cengage Learning.
